title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple and Mrs. McGillicuddy travel by taxi to Rutherford Hall. Despite the presence of the police, Miss Marple feels a sense of duty to investigate the possibility of further killings. Upon arriving at the estate, Miss Marple uses the pretext of needing to use the upstairs bathroom to gain entry to the house.

Once inside, Miss Marple introduces herself and Mrs. McGillicuddy to Emma and Cedric. During their conversation, Cedric mentions rumors that arsenic had been placed in the curry chicken and suggests that Lucy's aunt might have had knowledge of this. Miss Marple clarifies that her earlier mention of arsenic was only a guess.

Mr. Creakle-Soper enters the room and demands tea while complaining about rising taxes and the threat of socialists seizing property. Lucy and Brian arrive with tea and sandwiches to celebrate Dr. Campbell's birthday, though Mr. Creakle-Soper dismisses the occasion with disdain. During the gathering, Miss Marple remarks to Brian that he resembles a man she knew in St. Mary Mead named Ronnie Wells, a banker's son who lost his fortune in an East African cargo business, but Brian denies any relation. As Emma comments on the view from the window and Brian begins to explain the science behind the noise of jet planes, Mrs. McGillicuddy whispers to Emma that she is uncomfortable with Miss Marple's behavior. Miss Marple then asks for permission to use the upstairs bathroom.
